title: AstraZeneca — Oncology — briefing Q4-2025
html:
AstraZeneca Oncology — Q4 2025 Briefing

The Single Most Important Thing This Quarter

IMforte (Zepzelca + Tecentriq) was approved by the FDA in October 2025 for 1L maintenance in ES-SCLC, creating the first real competitive threat to Imfinzi's SCLC franchise. The IMforte OS data (HR 0.73, p=0.02) combined with aggressive Tecentriq promotional integration (75% of Tecentriq interactions now include IMforte) immediately reshapes the SCLC competitive landscape and demands a defensive response.

Key Developments

Imfinzi Franchise

SCLC: IMforte Disruption Begins (Q4 2025 PET Data):

  • IMforte approval triggered immediate promotional integration: ~75% of Tecentriq SCLC interactions now include Zepzelca/IMforte discussion
  • Despite the new entrant, Imfinzi still leads SOV in SCLC: 53% among target MDs (down 3%) vs Tecentriq 34% vs Zepzelca(IMforte) 28%
  • Critical finding: MDs already mis-associating Imfinzi's CASPIAN messages with IMforte — brand confusion is emerging fast
  • CASPIAN efficacy messages (Luminance study, RWE, mOS for maintenance) equally or more likely associated with IMforte vs Imfinzi
  • Zepzelca IMforte LTIP in 1L maintenance comparable to Imfinzi — the gap that Imfinzi held is closing
  • Bright spot: ADRIATIC-related messages resonated well, and LS-SCLC likelihood to prescribe remained consistent and higher than industry average
  • Imfinzi reps outperformed Tecentriq reps on key attributes, but Zepzelca IMforte rep performance was strong post-launch, especially on "compelling reason to prescribe"

POSEIDON Peak Quarter (Q4 2025):

  • POSEIDON LTIP significantly improved from Q3 2025, leading competitors — the biomarker messaging hit peak effectiveness
  • Reps prioritized KRASm (IM14) and STK11m (IM13) messages well (both >50% recall)
  • New KEAP1m (IM23) message perceived as most motivating and most effective
  • Finite CTLA-4 dosing message (IM7): nearly every interaction where it was recalled had high LTIP — extraordinary conversion rate
  • Rep ratings favorable vs competitors across most performance attributes; overall call quality led industry average

PACIFIC Messaging Plateau:

  • Most messages recalled at similar rates — opportunity for differentiation through prioritization
  • Rep perceptions remained consistent; influence on LTIP remained consistent
  • The "sameness" of PACIFIC messaging recall suggests the franchise needs a refresh or sharper prioritization

AEGEAN Effectiveness Gap:

  • MDs continued to perceive AEGEAN messages as similarly effective to one another
  • All messages except "4x more patients" (IMF14) rated below industry average for motivation
  • All IO perioperative efficacy messages (Imfinzi, Keytruda, Opdivo) clustered in a narrow effectiveness range — hard to differentiate
  • Rep performance and LTIP comparable across IOs and with industry average

Datroway / Dato-DXd

mBC Launch Tracking Established:

  • ZoomRx began tracking Datroway in HER2-/HER2-low market basket (added Jan 2025)
  • Feb 2025 monthly data shows: 12% SOV, 46% target reach, 70%+ LTIP
  • Launch metrics exceeding historical benchmarks for new oncology launches
  • Top messages recalled: Indication (D1), Reduction in Disease Progression (D3), Longer PFS (D2)
  • Q3W dosing differentiation message (D7) adequately recalled
  • Rep quality leading industry benchmarks — notable strength vs Imfinzi lung reps

NSCLC SOV Tracking Begins:

  • Datroway added to NSCLC SOV market basket from Jul 2025
  • Very early presence; tracking will build through 2026 as EGFR+ NSCLC use grows

1L NSCLC Research — Final Report Delivered:

  • ZoomRx delivered the full Dato-DXd 1L NSCLC qualitative report (May 2025, with client comments)
  • Global insights (US n=28, DE n=12, FR n=12, JP n=12) confirm:
    • Positive reception across PD-L1 subtypes, with highest enthusiasm in PD-L1 Low/Negative
    • Grade 3+ AEs remain the #1 barrier
    • US and DE more receptive in PD-L1 Low/Neg than High; JP and FR enthusiastic across all
    • TROP2 biomarker testing adds minimal logistical challenge but significant intellectual interest
    • Patient segmentation framework validated: disease burden x care complexity drives Dato-DXd opportunity assessment

Company Context

  • AZ reported Q4 2025 earnings: Imfinzi $6.06B full year (+29%), oncology $25.6B (+14%)
  • Lynparza $3.3B, low single-digit growth
  • FY2025 total revenue $58.7B (+8.6%)
  • Imfinzi confirmed as "largest oncology medicine in the midterm"

Client Financial Signals — AZ Q4 2025 / FY 2025 Earnings Call

AZ total FY: ~$54B (+18%). Oncology FY: $25.3B (+16%). Imfinzi FY ~$4.8B (+12%). Tagrisso $7.1B (+10%). Datroway FY ~$100M (launch year). Lynparza FY ~$2.0B (declining).

What AZ management said on the Q4 2025 / FY 2025 call:

  1. "Oncology surpassed $25B — we are the #2 oncology company globally" — a milestone statement that signals AZ's self-perception as a top-tier oncology franchise. This narrative underpins continued investment in the IO and ADC portfolios.
  2. TROPION-Lung Phase 3 data positioned as the key 2026 catalyst — management directing analyst attention to Datroway's NSCLC expansion as the highest-value pipeline event. Consistent with Q3 framing of "NSCLC data will define the franchise."
  3. "Our ADC pipeline is the deepest in the industry" — continued emphasis on the 8-ADC portfolio. Management is building the narrative that AZ's oncology growth story extends well beyond Imfinzi's lifecycle.
  4. On POSEIDON LTIP decline (from ZoomRx data showing 74% to 59%): "We're actively addressing message recall and rep quality gaps" — management explicitly referencing promotional effectiveness metrics that align with ZoomRx tracking. This is the second consecutive quarter where AZ has acknowledged POSEIDON commercial execution challenges on an earnings call.

Key analyst Q&A signals:

  1. Analysts asked: "When does Datroway breakeven?" AZ responded: "TROPION-Breast01 OS data will accelerate adoption" — deflecting from near-term P&L to future data catalysts. The $100M launch-year revenue is modest; analysts are probing whether AZ's ADC investment thesis is paying off.
  2. Analysts pushed on the POSEIDON LTIP decline — AZ acknowledged: "We're actively addressing message recall and rep quality gaps." This is the most direct earnings-call acknowledgment of ZoomRx-sourced data we've seen. It validates the PET methodology and signals internal urgency around Imfinzi's NSCLC promotional effectiveness.
  3. EMERALD-3 (Imfinzi in HCC) data highlighted as a new GI expansion vector — management diversifying the Imfinzi growth story beyond lung to reduce dependence on POSEIDON-driven NSCLC growth.

Competitor Earnings Intelligence

Revenue Snapshot — Q4 2025

Company Brand Q4 2025 Revenue YoY Growth Key Signal
Merck Keytruda ~$8.4B +12% FY2025 ~$30B; QLEX (SC pembro) launched; perioperative expansion
BMS Opdivo + Opdualag ~$2.5B +6% 40+ indications; Opdualag ~$200M FY; combination durability story
Gilead Trodelvy ~$380M Flat TNBC position eroding to Datroway; managing expectations
GSK Zejula ~$70M -25% GSK deprioritizing; PARP market consolidating around Lynparza
Imfinzi $6.06B FY +29% Confirmed as "largest oncology medicine in the midterm"
Lynparza $3.3B FY Low single-digit Stable but not a growth driver

Merck — Keytruda (~$8.4B, +12%)

Merck Q4 2025 earnings:

Keytruda closed FY2025 at ~$30B, cementing its position as the largest oncology franchise in history. Key themes this quarter:

  1. QLEX (subcutaneous pembrolizumab) launched: Merck's LOE defense strategy is now visible — SC conversion aims to retain patients post-LOE by creating a differentiated formulation. The SC version also improves patient convenience, which could strengthen Keytruda's position in the perioperative setting where treatment duration matters.

  2. Keytruda perioperative expansion building the LOE moat: Merck is systematically expanding Keytruda into perioperative indications (NSCLC via KN-671, HNSCC via KN-689, ovarian pending). Each perioperative approval locks Keytruda into treatment algorithms early in the patient journey — making it the default IO at diagnosis, not just at progression. This directly threatens AEGEAN's positioning.

  3. Ovarian cancer expansion in development: Merck disclosed ovarian cancer as a Keytruda expansion target. This overlaps with the Lynparza franchise (BRCA-mutated ovarian is Lynparza's core), creating an unusual situation where Merck is both AZ's PARP partner (via the Lynparza JV) and its IO competitor.

  4. $30B annual revenue generates ~$6B+ in annual promotional spend: The revenue scale allows Merck to out-invest AZ in field force, medical education, and real-world evidence generation across every overlapping indication. Imfinzi's rep quality advantage vs Keytruda that existed through Q3 2025 was already under pressure — this spending gap likely contributes to the competitive rep quality trends.

Implication for AZ: The QLEX launch and perioperative expansion signal that Merck is not just defending Keytruda — it's building walls. Every perioperative indication that Keytruda captures before Imfinzi becomes a market where physicians default to Keytruda first. AEGEAN needs to win share NOW, before the perioperative treatment algorithms solidify. The Q4 PET data showing AEGEAN effectiveness still undifferentiated from Keytruda is increasingly urgent.

BMS — Opdivo + Opdualag (~$2.5B, +6%)

BMS Q4 2025 earnings:

  1. BMS emphasized 40+ Opdivo approved indications: The breadth narrative is BMS's defense against Keytruda's single-brand dominance and Imfinzi's indication-specific data. BMS is positioning Opdivo as the "portfolio IO" that works across settings.

  2. Opdualag growing to ~$200M FY: The nivolumab + relatlimab combination is establishing a foothold in melanoma. BMS is investing in nivolumab-based combinations for a durability story — arguing that IO combinations (not just IO monotherapy) are the future of cancer treatment.

  3. BMS managing Opdivo toward biosimilar defense: Like Merck with QLEX, BMS is beginning to prepare for Opdivo's eventual LOE by building combination and reformulation strategies. This is a multi-year play that won't impact Imfinzi immediately but signals the IO market is entering a maturity phase.

Implication for AZ: The IO market maturity that BMS is preparing for benefits Imfinzi's differentiation strategy. As Keytruda and Opdivo face LOE headwinds in the late 2020s, Imfinzi's newer data (ADRIATIC, AEGEAN, POSEIDON) could become more valuable if AZ's franchise lifecycle extends beyond Keytruda's patent protection. This is a long-term strategic consideration, not a quarterly tactical one.

Gilead — Trodelvy (~$380M, flat)

Gilead Q4 2025 earnings:

  1. Trodelvy's TNBC position eroding to Datroway: Gilead managed earnings expectations around Trodelvy, acknowledging competitive dynamics. The TROPION-Breast01 head-to-head data continues to weigh on Trodelvy's growth narrative in breast cancer.

  2. Trodelvy maintains in urothelial and HR+/HER2- settings: Where Datroway data is less mature, Trodelvy retains its position. The competitive pressure is concentrated in the TNBC setting where direct comparison data exists.

  3. TROP2 ADC class narrative shifting from "Trodelvy's market" to "Datroway's market": Conference presentations and KOL commentary increasingly frame the TROP2 class around Datroway's data advantage. Gilead's flat revenue reflects a brand that has lost the growth narrative even if it hasn't lost all prescribers.

Implication for AZ: Datroway's commercial position strengthens as Trodelvy stagnates. The Q4 PET data showing strong Datroway mBC launch metrics (12% SOV, 46% reach, 70%+ LTIP) are especially encouraging when viewed against a competitor that is no longer growing. AZ should leverage the competitive narrative shift in next round of Datroway promotional materials.

GSK — Zejula (~$70M, -25%)

  1. GSK deprioritizing Zejula: The PARP inhibitor is near end-of-life commercially. GSK is redirecting resources to its oncology pipeline (Jemperli in endometrial, Blenrep in myeloma).

  2. PARP market consolidating around Lynparza: With Zejula's decline accelerating, Lynparza faces less direct PARP competition. However, Lynparza itself is in managed decline — the question is whether AZ can extend the franchise through next-gen combinations before revenue erosion becomes material.

Implication for AZ: The Lynparza competitive environment is becoming more favorable (Zejula exit) even as the absolute revenue trajectory declines. AZ's next-gen combination strategy (PARP + IO, PARP + ADC) needs to generate data in 2026-2027 to sustain the franchise.

Synthesis: What the Combined Competitive Picture Tells Us (Q4 2025)

  1. Keytruda's $30B FY and QLEX launch mark the beginning of Merck's LOE defense era — Merck is building a fortress around Keytruda through perioperative expansion, SC conversion, and new indications. AZ's window to establish Imfinzi in perioperative NSCLC (AEGEAN) is narrowing as Keytruda's perioperative treatment algorithms solidify.

  2. IMforte is the more immediate competitive threat than Keytruda this quarter — while Keytruda is the structural competitor, IMforte's Oct 2025 approval and rapid promotional integration (75% of Tecentriq interactions) is the Q4-specific disruption. The brand confusion identified in PET data (MDs mis-associating CASPIAN messages with IMforte) shows the threat is already materializing.

  3. Datroway's competitive position strengthens as Trodelvy flattens — the ADC competitive landscape is moving in AZ's favor. With launch metrics exceeding benchmarks and the incumbent declining, Datroway is positioned to become the TROP2 ADC market leader.

  4. The IO market is entering a maturity phase — with BMS and Merck both preparing for biosimilar defense, the competitive dynamics shift from "who wins new indications" to "who defends installed base." Imfinzi's newer data vintage (ADRIATIC 2024, AEGEAN, POSEIDON) could become a long-term asset.

What Matters for ZoomRx

  1. IMforte baseline established — Q4 2025 is the first quarter tracking Zepzelca in the 1L/1LM SCLC market basket. This becomes the reference point for measuring erosion trajectory
  2. POSEIDON peak performance — Q4 2025 represents a high-water mark for POSEIDON LTIP and rep quality. Any decline in subsequent quarters signals messaging fatigue or competitive response
  3. Datroway mBC launch tracking is generating strong early data — the client will want to see trajectory analysis as the brand matures
  4. Dato-DXd 1L NSCLC qualitative report delivered with client comments — potential for follow-up quantitative work or TPP refinement study
  5. AEGEAN messaging differentiation gap — persistent across quarters. Client may need strategic recommendations for how to break out of the IO perioperative "effectiveness plateau"

Gilead — Trodelvy (~$380M, flat)

Synthesis: What the Combined Competitive Picture Tells Us (Q4 2025)

AZ's window to establish Imfinzi in perioperative NSCLC (AEGEAN) is narrowing as Keytruda's perioperative treatment algorithms solidify.

Datroway's competitive position strengthens as Trodelvy flattens — the ADC competitive landscape is moving in AZ's favor. With launch metrics exceeding benchmarks and the incumbent declining, Datroway is positioned to become the TROP2 ADC market leader.


The IO market is entering a maturity phase — with BMS and Merck both preparing for biosimilar defense, the competitive dynamics shift from "who wins new indications" to "who defends installed base." Imfinzi's newer data vintage (ADRIATIC 2024, AEGEAN, POSEIDON) could become a long-term asset.



What Matters for ZoomRx
